claim: Context degradation is a gradient, not a cliff: models with 200K token windows show significant degradation starting at 50K tokens, well below the stated limit.
actionable: Monitor model performance before hitting stated context limits; test your use case at 25-50% of maximum context window to establish a safe operating range.
source_title: Context Engineering in 29 Minutes: Complete Course

claim: Lost in the middle: LLMs exhibit a U-shaped attention curve with 30+ percentage point accuracy drops when relevant information moves from the beginning to the middle of context.
actionable: In agent systems, place original instructions and essential context early in the context window; put dynamically-generated results and history later to avoid attention degradation.
source_title: Context Engineering in 29 Minutes: Complete Course

claim: The four core context engineering strategies are write (persist externally), select (retrieve only what's needed), compress (summarize and trim), and isolate (separate concerns via sub-agents).
actionable: Organize your context management approach using these four categories: write externally via files/memory, actively select relevant data at each step, compress history proactively, and use sub-agents for focused tasks.
source_title: Context Engineering in 29 Minutes: Complete Course

claim: Context poisoning occurs when hallucinations or errors enter context and get referenced in subsequent steps, compounding mistakes; fix via context pruning and validating external data before injection.
actionable: Implement validation checks on tool outputs before they enter context; actively remove outdated or conflicting information; compress failed attempt history rather than keeping it visible.
source_title: Context Engineering in 29 Minutes: Complete Course

claim: Tool confusion: agents given 40+ tool definitions become confused and make poor selections despite the context being within window limits; RAG MCP improved accuracy from 14% to 43% while halving tokens.
actionable: Use dynamic tool retrieval (RAG MCP) to surface only relevant tools per step rather than dumping all definitions; test tool selection accuracy at high cardinality.
source_title: Context Engineering in 29 Minutes: Complete Course

claim: Using the think tool in Claude improved performance by 54% on certain tasks by giving agents a dedicated scratchpad for intermediate work.
actionable: Provide agents with explicit scratchpad/note-taking tools; let them persist intermediate findings before context fills up.
source_title: Context Engineering in 29 Minutes: Complete Course

claim: System prompts for agents should sit at the sweet spot: specific enough to guide autonomous behavior but flexible enough to let the model apply judgment; too prescriptive breaks on edge cases, too vague provides no guidance.
actionable: Write system prompts that specify heuristics and decision criteria without hard-coded rules; provide diverse few-shot examples instead of trying to anticipate every edge case.
source_title: Context Engineering in 29 Minutes: Complete Course

claim: Kimi K2.6 demonstrated autonomous agent execution for 13 hours with 1000+ tool calls, modifying 4000 lines of code and nearly tripling throughput while being more cost-effective than previous approaches.
actionable: Evaluate long-horizon agentic models like Kimi K2.6 for tasks requiring extended autonomous execution without repeated restarts.
source_title: Context Engineering in 29 Minutes: Complete Course

claim: Frequent intentional compaction in multi-phase workflows keeps context below 40-60% capacity; each phase produces a compressed artifact that becomes the next phase's input with a fresh context window.
actionable: Structure agent work into phases with explicit boundaries: research (sub-agents explore), compaction (summarize to markdown), planning (clean context), implementation (follow plan), testing (compress results).
source_title: Context Engineering in 29 Minutes: Complete Course

claim: Claude Code uses hybrid retrieval (claw.md up front + just-in-time tools) plus auto-compaction at 95% context utilization and spawns sub-agents for complex tasks.
actionable: Load foundational rules via claw.md at session start; use grep/glob for on-demand code exploration; set up auto-compaction as a safety net at high thresholds.
source_title: Context Engineering in 29 Minutes: Complete Course

claim: KV cache stability: keeping context prefix stable between API calls enables cache reuse (30 cents per million tokens vs 3 dollars for uncached), a 10x cost difference relevant for agents making 30-40 calls per task.
actionable: Minimize changes to the early part of your context between API calls; use tool masking instead of removal to maintain prefix stability and cache reuse.
source_title: Context Engineering in 29 Minutes: Complete Course
